person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Brief Life History of John
When John Adams of Feene was born on 7 January 1472, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, his father, John Upton Avril Adams, was 24 and his mother, Jane Rannelegh, was 18. He married Elizabeth Sharlon on 9 March 1504, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. He died on 2 August 1557, in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85, and was buried in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Adams於大約1452年出生在英國英格蘭戴文達特姆斯時，他的父親Thomas Adams31歲，母親Jane Rannelegh-1歲。他於1504年3月9日與Elizabeth Sharlon在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛結婚。他去世於大約1484年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝福斯東享年34歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛。

nameMeanings.name: Adams
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Muslim (mainly Ethiopia) and Arabic: from the personal name Adem, a derivative (in Ethiopia) or a variant (among Arabs, e.g. in Algeria) of Arabic Ādam (see Adam 3). This form of the surname is also found among Christian Arabs (e.g. in Lebanon).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Adams was born about 1452, in Dartmouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Adams, was 31 and his mother, Jane Rannelegh, was -1. He married Elizabeth Sharlon on 9 March 1504, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. He died about 1484, in Beverston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34, and was buried in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Church of Saint David, Barton Saint Davi: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드
ko.lifeSummary: John Adams(이)가 대략 1452년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 더르트모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Adams(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Rannelegh(은)는 -1세였다. 그는 1504년 3월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 Elizabeth Sharlon(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 대략 1484년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 비버스턴에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Adams родился около 1452 г. в местности дартмоут, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Adams было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jane Rannelegh, -1. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Sharlon 9 марта 1504 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер около 1484 г. в местности бивирстон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет и был похоронен в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.
